title: Minuartia pseudoimbricata
taxonomy: Minuartia pseudoimbricata is classified within the family Caryophyllaceae. This classification places it within the order Caryophyllales. The family Caryophyllaceae is a group of flowering plants commonly known as the pink or carnation family. Minuartia pseudoimbricata is also classified as a species within the genus Minuartia. The genus Minuartia is a group of small, herbaceous plants commonly known as the sandworts.

intro: Minuartia pseudoimbricata, also known as the pseudo-imbricate pearlwort, is a low-growing, mat-forming alpine plant species belonging to the carnation family (Caryophyllaceae). This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine, and its health benefits are attributed to its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs. The bioactive constituents of Minuartia pseudoimbricata include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are thought to be responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Minuartia pseudoimbricata is commonly used to prepare infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat respiratory complaints and as a general tonic to boost immune function.
